mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for advanced features.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface (CLI) which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
This command deploys vLLM to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Deploy vLLM with the specified version.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the vLLM release.
> Deletes the entire cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This step will upgrade your vLLM deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get pods --namespace vllm

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or the registry is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the registry is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources allocated for the vLLM deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be exposed correctly or the application is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly configured and the application pods are running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods including INT4 and FP8, improved memory management with PagedAttention.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
